all.en: Six months ago, I could barely run for two minutes without stopping to catch my breath.
all.zh: 六個月前，我跑不到兩分鐘就得停下來喘氣。

all.en: My doctor said my cholesterol was creeping up, so I decided to take action right away.
all.zh: 醫生說我的膽固醇正在上升，所以我決定立刻採取行動。

all.en: I signed up for a 10K race that was scheduled for the end of October.
all.zh: 我報名了一場安排在十月底的十公里路跑。

all.en: That gave me exactly twenty weeks to go from couch potato to confident runner.
all.zh: 這給了我整整二十週的時間從沙發馬鈴薯變成有自信的跑者。

all.en: I downloaded a beginner training plan and printed it on a big sheet by the door.
all.zh: 我下載了一份初學者訓練計畫，列印成大張貼在門邊。

all.en: Week one was painful, and my calves ached every time I climbed the stairs.
all.zh: 第一週很痛苦，每次爬樓梯我的小腿都會酸。

all.en: My wife laughed and said I walked like a robot for the first ten days.
all.zh: 我太太笑說我前十天走路像機器人一樣。

all.en: By the third week, the soreness faded and the morning runs felt easier.
all.zh: 到了第三週，酸痛消退了，晨跑也變得比較輕鬆。

all.en: I started running before sunrise because the streets were quiet and the air was cool.
all.zh: 我開始在日出前跑步，因為街道安靜，空氣涼爽。

all.en: A neighbor named Mr. Chen often waved at me from his balcony as I passed.
all.zh: 有位姓陳的鄰居常從陽台向經過的我揮手。

all.en: He used to be a marathon runner and gave me tips about pacing and breathing.
all.zh: 他以前是馬拉松選手，給了我關於配速和呼吸的建議。

all.en: I learned to breathe in through my nose for three steps and out for two.
all.zh: 我學會用鼻子吸氣三步，再吐氣兩步。

all.en: My first long run of five kilometers ended with me sitting on a curb, grinning.
all.zh: 我第一次跑完五公里長跑時，坐在路緣上開心地咧嘴笑。

all.en: I posted a photo of my muddy shoes online, and old classmates sent encouragement.
all.zh: 我把沾滿泥的鞋子照片放上網，老同學都來鼓勵我。

all.en: Two of them said they had been thinking about starting to run as well.
all.zh: 其中兩位說他們也一直在考慮要開始跑步。

all.en: We made a group chat to share routes, playlists, and weekly mileage totals.
all.zh: 我們建了一個群組，分享路線、播放清單和每週的里程數。

all.en: Around week ten, I twisted my ankle on a loose brick in the park.
all.zh: 大約第十週時，我在公園被一塊鬆動的磚絆到扭傷腳踝。

all.en: The doctor told me to rest for two weeks and not to rush my recovery.
all.zh: 醫生告訴我要休息兩週，不要急著恢復訓練。

all.en: Watching my plan fall behind was frustrating, but I focused on stretching and core work.
all.zh: 看著計畫落後讓我很沮喪，但我把重心放在伸展和核心訓練上。

all.en: When I returned to the track, I kept my pace slow and listened to my body.
all.zh: 回到跑道後，我刻意放慢配速，仔細傾聽身體的訊號。

all.en: By week fifteen, eight kilometers no longer felt impossible, just tiring.
all.zh: 到了第十五週，八公里不再讓我覺得不可能，只是有點累。

all.en: I bought proper running shoes after a fitting at a specialty store downtown.
all.zh: 我在市區一家專賣店試穿後，買了一雙真正合腳的跑鞋。

all.en: The salesperson explained how my foot rolled inward and recommended a supportive model.
all.zh: 店員解釋我的腳會內旋，並推薦了一款有支撐力的鞋款。

all.en: The new shoes made a surprising difference in how my knees felt after long runs.
all.zh: 新鞋讓我長跑後膝蓋的感覺有了驚人的改善。

all.en: Two weeks before race day, I completed a nine-kilometer practice run in light rain.
all.zh: 比賽前兩週，我在小雨中完成了九公里的練習跑。

all.en: I felt strong at the finish, and that gave me the confidence I had been missing.
all.zh: 我跑完時感覺很有力量，這給了我一直缺少的自信。

all.en: The night before the race, I laid out my clothes, bib, and water bottle.
all.zh: 比賽前一晚，我把衣服、號碼布和水壺都擺好。

all.en: I barely slept, but the excitement carried me through the morning anyway.
all.zh: 我幾乎沒睡，但興奮感無論如何撐著我度過了早晨。

all.en: At the starting line, thousands of runners stretched and chatted under the cool sky.
all.zh: 在起跑線上，數千名跑者在涼爽的天空下伸展、聊天。

all.en: The starting horn echoed, and the crowd surged forward like a slow river.
all.zh: 起跑號角響起，人群像緩緩流動的河水般向前湧去。

all.en: I held back during the first three kilometers to avoid burning out too soon.
all.zh: 前三公里我刻意放慢，避免太早把力氣用完。

all.en: Around the seven-kilometer mark, my legs ached and my mind wanted to quit.
all.zh: 大約跑到七公里時，我的腿開始發痠，內心想放棄。

all.en: I thought of my training, my friends online, and the kind man on the balcony.
all.zh: 我想起我的訓練、線上的朋友，還有那位站在陽台的好心人。

all.en: When I crossed the finish line, the official clock read fifty-eight minutes flat.
all.zh: 我衝過終點線時，大會時鐘正好顯示五十八分整。

all.en: I had become a runner, and I already knew that a half-marathon would be next.
all.zh: 我已經成為一名跑者，而且我已經知道下一個目標會是半馬。

all.en: My wife met me at the finish line holding a paper sign with my name in glitter letters.
all.zh: 我太太在終點線等我，手上拿著一張用亮粉寫著我名字的紙板。

all.en: She handed me a banana, a bottle of cold coconut water, and a tight grateful hug.
all.zh: 她遞給我一根香蕉、一瓶冰涼的椰子水，並給了我一個感激的擁抱。

all.en: Mr. Chen had quietly come to watch and clapped from beside the railing without a word.
all.zh: 陳先生默默地來看我，從欄杆旁無聲地拍著手。

all.en: I jogged over to thank him properly, still shaking from the rush of finishing.
all.zh: 我輕步跑過去好好謝謝他，身體仍因衝過終點的激動而顫抖。

all.en: The following Monday, I signed up for a half-marathon scheduled for the next spring.
all.zh: 接下來的週一，我報名了隔年春天舉辦的半程馬拉松。

all.en: My online running friends agreed to register too, and we began planning meet-up long runs.
all.zh: 線上的跑友也一起報名，我們開始規劃見面一起練長跑。

all.en: Mr. Chen offered to coach me for free on weekends in exchange for one cup of coffee.
all.zh: 陳先生主動提出免費當我的週末教練，只要請他一杯咖啡作為回報。

all.en: He drew up a careful training plan that respected my old ankle injury and busy schedule.
all.zh: 他為我細心擬定訓練計畫，兼顧我舊腳踝傷勢和忙碌的行程。

all.en: Five months later, I finished the half-marathon in two hours and twelve minutes flat.
all.zh: 五個月後，我以兩小時十二分整完成半程馬拉松。

all.en: Crossing that second finish line, I finally understood that every starting line begins inside.
all.zh: 衝過第二條終點線時，我終於明白每一條起跑線都是從內心出發的。

all.en: After the half-marathon, my doctor reviewed my latest blood test results with a wide pleased smile.
all.zh: 半馬之後，醫生帶著燦爛滿意的笑容檢視我最新的血液檢查結果。

all.en: My cholesterol had returned to a healthy range, and my resting heart rate had dropped significantly.
all.zh: 我的膽固醇恢復到健康範圍，靜止心率也明顯下降。

all.en: She asked me to share my running story with her other patients during their checkups.
all.zh: 她請我在其他病人健檢時和他們分享我的跑步故事。

all.en: I started a small free beginner's running group that met at the park every Sunday morning.
all.zh: 我創立了一個免費的小型初學者跑步小組，每個週日早晨在公園集合。

all.en: Six strangers came on the first morning, all of them nervous and quietly hopeful about starting fresh.
all.zh: 第一個早晨來了六位陌生人，每個人都緊張卻又安靜地懷抱著重新開始的希望。

all.en: Within three months, the group had grown to twenty members from all walks of life together.
all.zh: 三個月內，這個小組成長到二十位來自各行各業的成員。

all.en: Mr. Chen volunteered as our unofficial coach and brought homemade rice balls to share after every session.
all.zh: 陳先生自願擔任我們的非正式教練，每次練習後都帶著自製飯糰來分享。

all.en: One year later, twelve of our members completed their own first 10K race together as a team.
all.zh: 一年後，我們小組中有十二位成員以團隊形式一起完成了他們自己的第一場十公里賽。

all.en: We took a group photo at the finish line, every face glowing with the same proud disbelief.
all.zh: 我們在終點線拍了一張團體照，每張臉都閃耀著同樣自豪而不敢置信的光芒。

all.en: I framed that photo and hung it beside my running shoes as a reminder of how far I had come.
all.zh: 我把那張照片裱框，掛在跑鞋旁邊，提醒自己一路走來有多遠。

all.en: Two years later, I signed up for my first full marathon scheduled for the following winter in Taipei.
all.zh: 兩年後，我報名了隔年冬天在台北舉辦的人生第一場全程馬拉松。

all.en: Mr. Chen had passed away peacefully that summer, and I dedicated my training journal to his memory.
all.zh: 陳先生那年夏天安詳辭世，我把我的訓練日誌獻給他作為紀念。

all.en: His daughter brought me his old running stopwatch, telling me he had wanted me to inherit it eventually.
all.zh: 他的女兒送來他的舊跑步碼錶，告訴我他生前希望這支錶最終由我繼承。

all.en: I wore it on every long training run, feeling oddly like he was still gently coaching from above.
all.zh: 我每次長跑訓練都戴著它，奇妙地感覺他仍在上方溫柔地指導著我。

all.en: Our beginner running group expanded into three neighborhoods and now hosts over fifty regular members weekly.
all.zh: 我們的初學者跑步小組擴展到三個社區，每週有超過五十位常態成員。

all.en: I trained five of them as group leaders so I could focus on coaching the slower newcomers myself.
all.zh: 我培訓了其中五位擔任小組長，讓我能專心指導跑得較慢的新成員。

all.en: On marathon morning, my wife and my daughter waited at three different cheer points along the route.
all.zh: 馬拉松當天早晨，我太太和女兒在沿路三個不同的加油點等著我。

all.en: I crossed the finish line in four hours and thirty-eight minutes, holding Mr. Chen's stopwatch tightly.
all.zh: 我以四小時三十八分通過終點線，緊握著陳先生那支碼錶。

all.en: That night, I sat on our balcony with cold barley tea and quietly thanked every single mile.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我坐在陽台上喝著冰麥茶，靜靜地感謝走過的每一英里。

all.en: Six months later, I started writing a book about how ordinary people can quietly become real runners.
all.zh: 六個月後，我開始撰寫一本書，主題是平凡人如何悄悄成為真正的跑者。
